title: brassica campestris var. glauca
intro: Brassica campestris var. glauca, commonly known as Chinese broccoli or Gai Lan, is a variety of broccoli that belongs to the Brassica family of plants. It has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which are thought to help lower cholesterol levels and improve cardiovascular health. The health benefits of Chinese broccoli include its ability to support immune function, reduce the risk of certain cancers, and aid in digestion, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. Chinese broccoli can be prepared in a variety of ways, including steaming, sautéing, and incorporating into soups and stir-fries, and is often available in health food stores and Asian markets in the form of fresh or frozen vegetables.
